trades.sentiment_summary: DHT Holdings stock recently hit new 52-week highs at 14.49 USD and 18.41 USD, reflecting strong upward momentum and investor confidence amid a 76% yearly gain. Recent fleet expansions include the delivery of VLCC newbuilding DHT Antelope.

Credo Technology Group (CRDO) reported exceptional Q3 2026 earnings on March 3, with revenue of $407 million (up 51.9% sequentially and 201.5% YoY) and EPS of $1.07, beating expectations, alongside a 68.6% gross margin and strong cash flow. Despite these robust results and positive guidance, the stock declined sharply post-earnings, dropping 14.81% to $97.30 on March 3 and continuing volatility, closing at $112.33 on March 10 after a 3.15% decline. Recent analyses highlight bullish fundamentals driven by AI demand but note investor concerns over high customer concentration and operating expenses.

Recent updates highlight Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) aggressively accumulating Ethereum, with holdings reaching 4.53 million ETH and cash at a record $1.2 billion as of March 9, positioning it for AI agents in the digital economy[2]. A March 10 video discussion notes a strategic shift including record ETH purchases and cash raises, though trading at low multiples to net asset value (MNAV), suggesting undervaluation[4]. No other major news events reported in the last 24 hours as of March 11, 2026. Analyst consensus remains a Buy with a $151.82 price target, though not tied to breaking news[1][5].

Wells Fargo lowered its price target on SLM Corp. to $30 from $35, citing lower gain-on-sale margins and higher operating expenses, while maintaining an Overweight rating[1]. Compass Point downgraded SLM from Buy to Sell with a $23 price target following the Investor Forum and updated earnings outlook[1]. SLM announced a $200 million accelerated share repurchase on March 9, 2026, and its CEO is speaking at the RBC conference today[4].

Recent stock prices show stability around $52-53 in early March 2026[3].
